text: or a sunset or some check-in dates that are mandated, which we have done before with policies on council. We say you have to come back and check with us before we take the next step. I think that's a great way to look at this, to try it out. And I do want to, you know, address some of the things that folks have told us and some of the signs you've been hearing saying energy choice. Like, I just want to say, like, when I have gotten into homes in Bend, I didn't have much of an energy choice right if I wanted more electric appliances that choice had been made for me right and so I think what we're trying to do is create an environment where there's just a little more parity right on these choices that are still going to exist and as we saw under the 20% fee which is probably what I would support five out of seven homes are probably still going to be gas homes, right? So we're not forcing something. We are nudging. We are sending a signal, which is sort of where we have landed as a council and why we're here with a fee, is trying to send a signal to start this transition, because it is a transition we need to make. And I think everybody here agrees that there is a transition that needs to be made in order to address climate change. It's just sort of the how we get there that's tough. I do want to also just state, just for folks who are listening, I know most of the people in this room know this, we are talking about new construction. We're not talking about people's existing homes. We're not talking about people having to change their homes that they live in at all. So I just want to make sure that that's really clear. And this doesn't apply to commercial, and it doesn't apply to multifamily, right? Which multifamily has already moved this way on its own, right? It's the single family smaller homes that have not yet. So I think that's where I land is I am very cognizant of the affordability. I agree with Steve, 9,000 is way too high. I'm looking at something like 20% or even a little lower. I was looking through our fee schedule. All the other fees that homes have, there are some that are higher than that, there are some that are lower. I think part of the work of the accompanying incentive program is taking a look at some of these other ways that we can reward people who are making choices to have more efficient homes and homes that are better for sustainability of growth and bend But I think that's where I land is around 20% or less. And from my notes, we've got a couple people on the higher side of that and a couple people on the lower side of that. So that seems like a decent compromise. But I do need to hear from council. If we are ready to move forward with this fee, we need to make a decision for our community so they know what decision we've made, and we need to move forward with the next parts of this policy discussion, including what the timeline is for implementation, what any exemptions might be. But I think we want to send the staff a clear message of what it is we want to do, and we're not quite there yet. So can I see? Okay, so we have two people on the 50%, so we do not have a majority, I don't think, to go to 50% on this. Councilor Riley had suggested 33%. Who would support?
